mission.title: kubevirt: Allow for customization of virt-operator created deployments, daemonset
mission.description: Allow for customization of virt-operator created deployments, daemonset. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kubevirt deployment
steps.description:
Verify your virtctl version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
helm list -n kubevirt 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubevirt installation.

steps.title: Review kubevirt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevirt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get configmap -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevirt
```
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

**What you expected to happen**:

Allow arbitrary json patches to be provided to virt-operator in order to customize virt-handler daemonset, virt-api deployment, and virt-controller deployment.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow for customization of virt-operator created…
steps.description:
If Kubelet is not using a standard --root-dir, virt-handler
won't detect containerdisks properly. This moves from a
hard-coded "/var/lib/kubelet/pods" path to a configurable
one so virt-handler's paths can match the Kubelet's view.

There's a separate issue (edit: #3365) for exposing configurables like these through the operator, via merging JSON docs. I don't suspect this will be usable for most until this happens, unless they edit the daemonset after deployment.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get events -n kubevirt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow for customization of virt-operator created…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
If Kubelet is not using a standard --root-dir, virt-handler
won't detect containerdisks properly. This moves from a
hard-coded "/var/lib/kubelet/pods" path to a configurable
one so virt-handler's paths can match the Kubelet's view.

There's a separate issue (edit: #3365) for exposing configurables like these through the operator, via merging JSON docs.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevirt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
